summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with a passionate call to never lose hope, anchoring the message in Psalm 119:114: 'I have put my hope in your word.' He emphasized that no matter the difficulty—be it brokenness, sickness, or spiritual attack—there is always hope because God is a way-maker, healer, and redeemer. The core of the sermon was an exposition of Romans 12:12: 'Be joyful in hope, patient in affliction, faithful in prayer.' Pastor Travis explained that being joyful in hope means looking ahead to God's promises, with Jesus Himself being our 'blessed hope.' He warned that the enemy seeks to steal our joy and hope, but we must remain alert and trust in God's good name. The second point focused on being patient in affliction, drawing on James 1 to teach that trials test and mature our faith. Using the biblical analogies of storms, valleys, and fires, Pastor Travis assured the congregation that God is with us in every difficulty, refining us as in a furnace. He illustrated this with the story of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, highlighting that God walks with us in the fire. The final point was a call to be faithful in prayer, not as a religious duty but as a vital relationship with God. He shared scriptures on the power of prayer from Matthew 18 and James 5, stressing that prayer is powerful and effective. The service concluded with an extended time of prayer and anointing, inviting the congregation to receive breakthrough and miracles in every area of life.
mainPoints: Be joyful in hope by fixing your eyes on Jesus, our blessed hope, and refusing to let the enemy steal your joy.
Be patient in affliction, understanding that God uses trials, storms, and fires to refine you and grow your spiritual maturity.
Be faithful in prayer, cultivating it as a powerful, relational conversation with God that brings breakthrough and healing.

versesMentioned.text: You are my refuge and my shield; I have put my hope in your word.
Be joyful in hope, patient in affliction, faithful in prayer.
For I know the plans I have for you,” declares the Lord, “plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future.
May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you trust in him, so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding;
Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, To God’s elect, exiles scattered throughout the provinces of Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia and Bithynia,
while we wait for the blessed hope—the appearing of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ,
For what you have done I will always praise you in the presence of your faithful people. And I will hope in your name, for your name is good.

Therefore God exalted him to the highest place and gave him the name that is above every name, that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, in heaven and on earth and under the earth, and every tongue acknowledge that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.
Be alert and of sober mind. Your enemy the devil prowls around like a roaring lion looking for someone to devour.

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.
Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and will call him Immanuel.

When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and when you pass through the rivers, they will not sweep over you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned; the flames will not set you ablaze.
See, I have refined you, though not as silver; I have tested you in the furnace of affliction.

The satraps, prefects, governors and royal advisers crowded around them. They saw that the fire had not harmed their bodies, nor was a hair of their heads singed; their robes were not scorched, and there was no smell of fire on them.
When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.
In fact, though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you the elementary truths of God’s word all over again. You need milk, not solid food!
Therefore let us move beyond the elementary teachings about Christ and be taken forward to maturity, not laying again the foundation of repentance from acts that lead to death, and of faith in God,

Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven. Again, truly I tell you that if two of you on earth agree about anything they ask for, it will be done for them by my Father in heaven. For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them.

Is anyone among you in trouble? Let them pray. Is anyone happy? Let them sing songs of praise. Is anyone among you sick? Let them call the elders of the church to pray over them and anoint them with oil in the name of the Lord. And the prayer offered in faith will make the sick person well; the Lord will raise them up. If they have sinned, they will be forgiven. Therefore confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.
If you declare with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: Psalm 119:114 (NIV) - 'You are my refuge and my shield; I have put my hope in your word.'
Monday.focus: Anchoring your hope in God's word.
Monday.action: Write down one situation where you feel hopeless. Read Psalm 119:114 aloud three times, declaring your hope is in God's word.
Monday.prayer: Lord, my hope is in You and Your unchanging word. Be my refuge and shield today. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: Romans 12:12 (NIV) - 'Be joyful in hope, patient in affliction, faithful in prayer.'
Tuesday.action: Identify one promise of God you can be joyful about. Share that reason for joy with one other person.
Tuesday.prayer: God of hope, fill me with joy as I trust in Your promises. Help me to be joyful in hope today. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: James 1:2-4 (NIV) - 'Consider it pure joy... whenever you face trials...'
Wednesday.action: Reflect on a current challenge. Write down one way God might be using it to build perseverance or character in you.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, help me to see my trials from Your perspective. Give me patience and let this difficulty produce maturity in me. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: Isaiah 43:2 (NIV) - 'When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned...'
Thursday.focus: Trusting God's presence in the fire.
Thursday.action: Memorize Isaiah 43:2. When you face a stressful moment today, silently recall this verse.
Thursday.prayer: Immanuel, thank You for being with me in every storm and fire. I trust that You are with me right now. Amen.

Friday.scripture: Matthew 18:20 (NIV) - 'For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them.'
Friday.focus: The power of agreement in prayer.
Friday.action: Text or call one other believer and agree with them in prayer for a specific need.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, thank You for the promise of Your presence when we pray together. Unite our hearts in faith as we seek You. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: James 5:16 (NIV) - 'The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.'
Saturday.action: Set aside 10 minutes for uninterrupted prayer. Talk to God about your day, then listen quietly for a few moments.
Saturday.prayer: Heavenly Father, I come to You not with recited words, but with an open heart. Make my prayers powerful and effective as I seek Your face. Amen.
